steps.title: Distribute the subtraction
steps.content:
Start with $$\left(\frac{1}{2}x+3\right)-\left(\frac{2}{3}x-5\right).$$ Change the signs in the second parentheses:
$$\frac{1}{2}x+3-\frac{2}{3}x+5.$$

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content: The constants give $3+5=8$. The coefficient of $x$ is $$\frac{1}{2}-\frac{2}{3}=\frac{3}{6}-\frac{4}{6}=-\frac{1}{6}.$$ So the expression is $$-\frac{1}{6}x+8.$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: That matches choice A, so A is correct. The common-denominator step shows why the $x$ coefficient is negative.
